creator: rrghphar@bigpond.net.au
text:
Dear Jean-Yves,
 
although I was in the process of taking over from Ying-Chieh Chen, I'm happy
to stand aside on graphics/osg if you'd like to take over on all three.  I
had updated openthreads and openproducer to the OSG-1.0 level already
(PR/94998 and PR/98426 respectiv ely), and there is an open PR on OSG itself
(PR/105396).  After that one passed through, I was intending to do (mostly)
as you've already done :-).
 
However, I had not thought to implement a bsd.osg.mk for the common bits,
so you clearly have a deeper level of understanding of the port process
than I.  Also, if it makes your current work easier, then by all means be
my guest.  I'm just glad there's s omeone else in the world using OSG under
FreeBSD (the survey on the OSG website seemed to indicate a _very_ small
fraction of a percentage of BSD users, despite the fact that OSG compiles
"out of the box" under BSD, and has for a while now).
 
Are you a subscriber to the OSG-submissions list?  I don't recall seeing
your name there recently.  I was the one submitting patches to the main
OSG source tree to make it easier to build under FreeBSD.  If you don't do
it first, I'll take a close look at your patches and see if any are
appropriate for the "non-ports" build process, and if so, put them in the
tree for OSG-1.3.
 
Regards,
Matthew May
